skill.name: citation-network
skill.description: Build and visualize a citation network from a source/target CSV to identify key papers, communities, and emerging hotspots; use when you have citation pairs and need fast literature review or trend analysis.
skill.summary: Transform citation relationship data into interactive network graphs to identify influential papers and research communities.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 79 static findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The skill reads citation CSV files, computes network metrics locally using networkx, and generates output files (GEXF, JSON, HTML). The HTML includes a CDN link to vis-network for browser visualization. No credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious behavior detected. File operations are scoped to isolated run directories with path traversal protection.

low_findings.title: CDN Dependency in Generated HTML
low_findings.description: The HTML visualization includes a script tag loading vis-network from unpkg.com CDN at scripts/export_gexf_html.py:120. This is a legitimate visualization library dependency and is standard practice for self-contained HTML exports. The HTML file is opened locally in a browser and does not transmit data.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 20 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' across multiple files. No cryptographic operations exist in any file. The scanner likely matched on hash-related metadata fields (e.g., content_hash, tree_hash) in audit result JSON and variable names in documentation. All instances are false positives.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner: Windows SAM Database (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged the variable name SAMPLE_DATA in scripts/init_run.py:27 and its usage on line 50. This variable contains sample citation data used for testing the init workflow. It has no relation to Windows Security Account Manager. False positive with high confidence.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged datetime.now().strftime() calls used to generate timestamp-based run directory names. This is a standard pattern for creating unique, reproducible output directories and is not system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged 47 instances of backtick usage in SKILL.md. All instances are markdown inline code formatting syntax or fenced code blocks used for documentation purposes. They show users how to run commands manually. No automated command execution exists.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner: Dangerous Heuristic Combination (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The heuristic combined code execution, network, and credential access signals to flag a dangerous pattern. The code execution signal came from markdown documentation, the network signal from a legitimate CDN URL, and the credential access signal from the SAMPLE_DATA false positive. No actual dangerous combination exists.

content.user_title: Map citation networks and discover research trends
content.value_statement: This skill transforms source-target citation data into interactive network graphs. Researchers can identify influential papers, detect research communities, and find emerging hotspots from their citation data.
actual_capabilities: Build a directed citation graph from a CSV file containing source and target citation pairs
Export network files in GEXF format for analysis in Gephi and other graph tools
Generate interactive HTML visualizations for browser-based network exploration
Calculate basic network metrics including node count, edge count, and graph density
Identify the top 10 most-cited papers through degree centrality ranking
Create timestamped and isolated run directories for reproducible analysis workflows
limitations: Only reads source and target columns from the input CSV; additional metadata columns are ignored
Does not perform full-text search or paper recommendations from external databases
Network visualization requires an internet connection to load the vis-network library from CDN
Limited to basic degree centrality; advanced metrics like betweenness are not computed

use_cases.title: Map citations for a literature review
use_cases.description: Upload a CSV of citation pairs from your research area and get an interactive network graph showing which papers are most cited and how research communities connect.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate students and researchers conducting literature reviews

use_cases.title: Analyze team publication patterns
use_cases.description: Load citation data from your research group to visualize publication relationships, identify collaboration gaps, and spot emerging focus areas within the team.
use_cases.target_user: Lab directors and research group managers

use_cases.title: Study interdisciplinary citation flows
use_cases.description: Examine citation patterns across journals or disciplines to evaluate how knowledge flows between fields and which papers bridge different research communities.

prompt_templates.title: Quick start with citation data
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a CSV file with citation pairs (source and target columns). Help me initialize a run directory, set up the configuration, and build a citation network. Walk me through the steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user who has citation data ready and wants to get started quickly

prompt_templates.title: Analyze key papers and metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: I have built my citation network. Show me the basic network metrics and tell me which papers have the highest degree centrality. Explain what the density value means for my dataset.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user who has built a network and needs help interpreting the output metrics

prompt_templates.title: Export for publication or sharing
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to export my citation network for sharing with colleagues. Help me generate the GEXF file for Gephi analysis and the interactive HTML visualization for browser viewing.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user who needs to share results or perform deeper analysis in external tools

prompt_templates.title: Full workflow with large dataset
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a large citation dataset with over 5000 edges. Guide me through initializing the run, configuring encoding options, building the graph, computing metrics, and exporting to all available formats.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user working with a large dataset who needs the complete workflow

output_examples.input: Process a citations.csv file with 50 paper pairs and generate network analysis
output_examples.output: A network metrics summary showing 45 unique papers, 50 citation relationships, network density of 0.025, and a ranked list of the top 5 most-cited papers by degree centrality

output_examples.input: Generate an interactive visualization of the citation network
output_examples.output: An HTML file with an interactive network graph where nodes represent papers and directed arrows show citation direction. Users can zoom, pan, hover for paper labels, and search the network in a browser

best_practices: Use DOI-based identifiers for source and target columns to ensure correct node deduplication across your dataset
Always run init_run.py first to create an isolated run directory before building the network
Verify CSV column headers match the configured source and target column names before running the build script
anti_patterns: Do not place input files outside the run directory structure; the path scoping check will reject them
Do not use author names or paper titles as identifiers if papers may have similar names; use unique DOIs instead
Do not skip the init step and run build scripts directly; the workflow requires an initialized run directory

faq.question: What file format does the input need to be in?
faq.answer: The input must be a CSV file with source and target columns. Each row represents one citation from a source paper to a target paper.

faq.question: Can I analyze non-citation relationships with this tool?
faq.answer: Yes. Any directed relationship data works, including co-authorship links, hyperlink networks, or referral patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill search external databases for papers?
faq.answer: No. It only processes the citation data you provide. It does not search PubMed, Google Scholar, or any external source.

faq.question: What software do I need to install?
faq.answer: You need Python 3.10 or later with pandas and networkx libraries installed. The HTML visualization uses a CDN library loaded in the browser.

faq.question: How does the tool handle duplicate papers?
faq.answer: It deduplicates nodes by their identifier string. Using DOIs as identifiers gives the most reliable deduplication results.

faq.question: Can I use the outputs with other analysis tools?
faq.answer: Yes. The GEXF format is compatible with Gephi, Cytoscape, and many other network analysis applications.
